element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Single press for jump, repeated presses enable flight/swimming
B button attack; varies by magic level; degraded on hit
Scattered items replenish magic; losing magic level reduces attack capability
Scattered items replenish health; losing all health results in life loss
Regular back-and-forth patrol; shootable with magic attack
Stationary or moving hazards; avoidable or destroyable
Fire projectiles; destroyable with magic attack
Airborne patrolling enemies; engage flying mechanic
Eight unique bosses; each world culminates in boss encounter
Falling past bottom of level results in life loss; requires platform navigation
Running out of time results in life loss; creates pacing pressure

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Steady with escalating enemy density; nine worlds create progressive difficulty curve; boss encounters mark distinct tension peaks at world endings

difficulty_curve.early_game: Simple mechanics introduction with basic enemy types (moles); straightforward platforming; emphasis on learning jump and attack systems
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Intermediate complexity with multiple enemy types (cannons, tree trunks, birds); magic system depth increases; platforming sections lengthen
difficulty_curve.late_game: High complexity with combined hazards; water segments introduce fish enemies and swimming mechanic mastery requirements; boss difficulty escalates
notable_spikes: Boss encounters at each world ending
Introduction of bird enemies requiring airborne combat
Final world bosses combining multiple threat types

notable_level_patterns.description: Nine worlds, each containing platforming sections with enemy encounters and culminating in a boss fight
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Generic world pattern (specific level names not documented)

notable_level_patterns.description: Regular back-and-forth patrol patterns; enemies follow consistent rhythms enabling prediction; density increases through world progression
Early worlds: basic platforming over pits with few enemies; mid worlds: mixed hazards (cannons, obstacles); late worlds: dense enemy combinations with environmental threats
Magic level directly gates combat effectiveness; item collection creates risk-reward (health vs magic power balance)
